big_picture:
The Rajya Sabha has cleared the Finance Bill 2026, with the excise duty cut aimed at not burdening citizens, according to the Finance Minister. The opposition has termed this move as a poll tactic. 

Prime Minister Modi has reviewed fuel and fertilizer supplies amid the escalating conflict in West Asia. The review involved interactions with Chief Ministers to assess preparedness and plans. Iran has allowed two more LPG tankers from India to cross the Strait of Hormuz. The stock market has fallen by 1% and the rupee has dropped past 94/$ due to the Iran war and crude oil prices. 

The Delhi Police has busted an illegal arms manufacturing unit in Meerut, Uttar Pradesh, and arrested two key accused, including the main supplier. 

Mamata Banerjee has warned that the BJP will lose national power if it targets Bengal. She claimed that if the BJP comes to power in the state, they will use bulldozers and throw everyone out. 

The Parliament has passed the SHANTI Bill, Sustainable Harnessing and Advancement of Nuclear Energy for Transforming India Bill, 2025, as part of India's transition to clean energy ambitions.

The Union Government has notified the provisions of the National Sports Governance Act, 2025, aimed at regulating sports governance in India.

Legal.summary:
The Delhi High Court has set aside a Single Judge's order refusing to entertain a writ petition challenging the impounding of a passport, citing serious civil consequences. 

The Delhi High Court dismissed a claim for railway accident compensation, upholding the rejection of the claim even though the claimant had lost both hands in the accident, stating that no compensation can be given without proof of bona fide travel.

The Parliament passed the SHANTI Bill- Sustainable Harnessing and Advancement of Nuclear Energy for India's transition to clean energy ambitions, but the bill contains a missing link regarding shared liability.

Rajya Sabha has cleared the Finance Bill 2026, with the Finance Minister stating that the excise duty cut is to not burden citizens, a move the opposition has called a poll tactic. 

Amit Shah is set to release the BJP's list of charges against the TMC on March 28, highlighting the party's alleged failures in 14 sectors.

Lok Sabha has passed the Finance Bill 2026 with 32 amendments, sending it to Rajya Sabha, without introducing any new tax changes.

India's export policy on strategic minerals has been ruled to fall outside of competition law due to its sovereign function by the NCLAT Delhi. 

India has retained its retail inflation target at 4 percent for the next five years. 

India has cautioned World Trade Organization (WTO) members against using transparency as a means to justify trade retaliation.

Elon Musk participated in a call with Modi and Trump regarding the West Asia crisis, with the White House describing the talks as "productive". 

The West Asian conflict has had a significant impact on global monetary policy, with major central banks pausing rate cuts due to the US-Iran war. 

PM Modi has asked states to stay alert on supplies and prices amid the West Asia crisis, ensuring supply stability and adequate fuel availability.

The Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train project requires the B28 trainset for its 2027 launch.

The analysis suggests current measures fail to capture the true effectiveness of insurance in shielding people from economic shocks.

Iran has stated that vessels from "friendly" nations, including India, will be permitted passage, potentially easing fuel supply concerns.

articles.title: CSR spends up but aspirational districts fail to get bigger share
articles.summary: Corporate social responsibility spending by Indian companies has surged significantly over the last decade. However, a recent study reveals that aspirational districts, facing developmental challenges, have received only a small fraction of this investment. While over ₹1.22 lakh crore has been spent on CSR, just 12% reached these targeted areas, indicating an uneven distribution of funds.

articles.title: Sugar feels the heat as eateries cook less
articles.summary: India's sugar sales are down this month. Restaurants and hotels are cooking less because of LPG supply issues. This means sugar mills are not meeting their government-set sales quotas. March usually sees lower sugar demand after the holiday season. Global sugar exports are also slow due to market issues and higher costs.

articles.title: At WTO meet, Piyush Goyal says plurilateral agreements must not be imposed
articles.summary: India urged consensus on WTO plurilateral outcomes and careful reconsideration of the e-commerce duty moratorium. The US wants the moratorium made permanent. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal also called for restoring the dispute settlement system and effective special and differential treatment. Discussions are ongoing at the WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference in Cameroon.

articles.title: Gulf conflict puts India's FY27 growth at risk
articles.summary: India's economy faces pressure from the West Asia conflict. Economists predict a growth slowdown for FY27. Higher crude oil prices and supply issues are impacting stability. A prolonged conflict could significantly alter growth and inflation.

articles.title: OECD lowers India’s FY27 growth outlook to 6.1% amid global uncertainty
articles.summary: The OECD has lowered India's FY27 growth forecast to 6.1% due to global uncertainty and rising energy prices, despite expected support from reduced US tariffs. Inflation is projected to rise sharply to 5.1% in FY27, prompting the OECD to anticipate a temporary policy rate hike in India during Q2 2026.

However, the industry will need additional subsidy support.

articles.title: Kill switch soon for all digi payments to combat fraud
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Payments Vision 2028 introduces a shared liability model for unauthorized digital transactions, placing responsibility on both customer and beneficiary banks. The RBI also plans to expand the 'switch on/off' feature, currently for cards, to all digital payment modes, empowering users and enhancing ecosystem trust.

Doha Bank had launched its India operations in June 2014.

India stands with Bangladesh in its desire for accountability for these historical events.

articles.title: Credit-deposit ratio of banks touches a record 83%
articles.summary: Credit growth outpaced deposit mobilisation through the current financial year, with incremental credit at Rs 25.3 lakh crore exceeding incremental deposits of Rs 24.3 lakh crore. As a result, the incremental credit-deposit ratio stood at 103.9%. Historically, an 80% credit-deposit ratio is seen as healthy as it factors in the 3% of bank deposits that have to be maintained as cash reserves (CRR) and 18% in liquid govt bonds (statutory liquidity ratio)
